title: ADR-0023: DeprovisionWorkflow ownership — orvex-workflows as the durable Temporal control plane, engine-API executor seam, ruling-7 delete-propagation boundary
verdict_summary: Draft ADR (pending PO ratification) that records the seam decisions for the already-landed ENG-1424 (PR #6 orvex-studio-workflows; cites landed code, TestNoDirectSatelliteDelete, TestWorkflowID_*, unexported workflowID). Every build obligation it names maps to covered orvex-studio-workflows FR ids: FR-W14 (central Temporal control plane, covered), FR-W3/FR-W18 (DeprovisionWorkflow disable->grace->purge + entitlement revoke, covered), FR-W6/A-W1 (dispatcher already-started ACK, covered), plus the 'Studio provision/deprovision step API' and 'Two-phase deprovision soft-disable + grace-gated purge' stories. The engine POST /api/clerk/deprovision executor and WORKSPACE_DELETED emit already exist in the fork and stay engine-owned by ruling 7 (no new build). OQ-W6 (trigger-source ownership) is uncovered in covers-union but is an explicit OPEN QUESTION the ADR declines to hard-wire, tracked by ENG-1446 (clerk-identity, blocked-by) — a definition-phase/deferred item, not a build mandate from this ADR. Zero build gaps.

components.name: orvex-workflows (central Temporal control plane / durable-workflow home)
components.quote: orvex-workflows is the durable-workflow home for DeprovisionWorkflow. It is the central Temporal control plane for the Studio family (all durable workflows live here; activities call satellite APIs — no per-service Temporal workers).

components.name: workflows.DeprovisionWorkflow (workflow-tier orchestration artifact)
components.quote: DeprovisionWorkflow(ctx, DeprovisionInput) (DeprovisionResult, error) is a workflow-tier artifact (CS §6): liveness/orchestration only — retry policy (deprovisionActivityOptions: 1s->1m exponential back-off), grace-period workflow.Sleep, and the version-gated billing step.

components.name: Activities.CallWikiDeprovision (activity tier)
components.quote: Activities.CallWikiDeprovision (activity tier — the only place side-effecting IO happens) issues POST {WikiURL}/api/clerk/deprovision with Authorization: Bearer {WikiOrgToken} against the engine's existing clerk-deprovision.controller.ts executor.

components.name: CallStudioDeprovision (best-effort studio leg activity)
components.quote: the studio leg (CallStudioDeprovision) is best-effort and its failure alone does not fail the workflow.

components.name: engine POST /api/clerk/deprovision executor (engine-resident, already exists in fork)
components.quote: The engine API stays the sole executor; the workflow calls it, never reimplements it... All purge domain logic (who to disable, what to purge, storage cleanup) stays engine-resident behind this HTTP seam (CS §7).

components.name: TestNoDirectSatelliteDelete (guard test enforcing no store/DB import under internal/workflows/)
components.quote: the workflow does not fan out cross-DB deletes itself and holds no store/DB driver import (verified by absence: TestNoDirectSatelliteDelete greps internal/workflows/ for store/DB imports — none).

components.name: workflowID dedup logic in cmd/dispatcher/main.go
components.quote: The id is derived by the unexported workflowID in cmd/dispatcher/main.go (deprov-<type>-<key>), keying client.StartWorkflowOptions.ID so a concurrent duplicate start under the same id is rejected by Temporal server-side ID reuse.

components.name: cmd/dispatcher (CloudEvent ingress -> ExecuteWorkflow)
components.quote: the dispatch contract (decode CE -> build DeprovisionInput -> ExecuteWorkflow) holds for whichever source ultimately emits the CloudEvent.
